metaTitle: Drag to Resize Table Columns and Rows | {{BRAND_NAME}}
metaDescription: Resize table columns and rows by dragging — exactly like Excel, directly in the browser. Double-click to auto-fit. Free, no signup.
heroHeadline: Resize columns and rows the way Excel does.
heroSubtext: Drag any column border to resize it. Double-click to auto-fit to content. Tablesmit gives you precise layout control without touching a pixel value.

benefits.heading: Drag to resize
benefits.body: Hover over any column or row border. The cursor changes to a resize handle. Drag to the width or height you need — smooth, instant, 60fps.

benefits.heading: Double-click to auto-fit
benefits.body: Double-click any column border to auto-fit the column width to the longest content in that column. One gesture — perfect width.

benefits.heading: Resize rows too
benefits.body: Row height is also resizable by dragging the bottom border of any row. Use this for multi-line cell content or visual emphasis.

benefits.heading: Precise constraints
benefits.body: Columns have a minimum width of 60px and a maximum of 600px. Rows have a minimum of 32px. No cell ever becomes unreadable.

steps.heading: Hover over a column border
steps.body: Move the cursor to the right edge of any column header. The cursor changes to a horizontal resize indicator.

steps.heading: Drag to resize
steps.body: Click and drag to the width you want. A vertical guide line shows the new position as you drag. The column updates when you release.

steps.heading: Or double-click to auto-fit
steps.body: Double-click the column border to automatically set the width to match the widest content in that column.

useCases: Widening a description column to avoid text truncation in exports
Narrowing ID or code columns that need minimal space
Auto-fitting all columns before PDF export for clean layout
Adjusting row heights for tables with multi-line cell content
